title: 11 Best Herbal Teas For Edema
intro: Herbal teas for Edema are a type of herbal remedy that helps to reduce swelling and alleviate symptoms associated with this condition. Edema is a medical condition characterized by the accumulation of fluid in the body's tissues, often caused by poor circulation, kidney problems, or hormonal imbalances. Herbal teas can effectively treat Edema due to their natural diuretic and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to flush out excess fluids and reduce swelling. Examples of herbal teas that can help alleviate Edema symptoms include Dandelion root tea, which acts as a natural diuretic to remove excess fluids; Ginger tea, which reduces inflammation and improves circulation; Peppermint tea, which helps to relax blood vessels and reduce fluid buildup; and Cleaver's herb tea, which has been traditionally used to treat water retention and alleviate swelling.

preparations.herb_desc: Achillea millefolium teas have been traditionally used to treat edema, a condition characterized by swelling due to excess fluid retention. The herbal preparation's properties, such as anti-inflammatory and diuretic effects, help to reduce swelling and alleviate discomfort. The bioactive constituents, including sesquiterpene lactones and flavonoids, contribute to its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which aid in reducing inflammation and promoting fluid balance. The benefits of using Achillea millefolium teas to treat edema include reduced swelling, improved circulation, and enhanced overall well-being, making it a natural and effective remedy for this common ailment.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh or dried Achillea millefolium leaves.
Measure 1 tablespoon of the leaves and place them in a heat-resistant cup.
Pour 8 ounces of boiling water over the leaves and let it steep for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea into another cup using a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve to remove the leaves.
Drink 1 cup of the tea 2-3 times a day to help alleviate edema symptoms.
preparations.preparation_study: According to "Zeitschrift fur Naturforschung. C, Journal of biosciences", Achillea millefolium teas for edema may be effective in reducing inflammation and edema due to its chloroform extract, which includes stigmasterol and beta-sitosterol, as it significantly reduced mouse ear edema by up to 82% in an acute model.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Achillea millefolium teas can be used to treat edema, but it may cause side effects such as dizziness, stomach upset, and allergic reactions in some individuals. When using Achillea millefolium teas to treat edema, take precautions to avoid prolonged use, as it may interact with certain medications and exacerbate underlying health conditions, and also be cautious when consuming it with other herbal remedies or foods that may have similar properties.
preparation_usage: Consult a doctor before using Achillea millefolium tea for edema treatment.
Drink 1 cup of tea 2-3 times a day, for 7-10 days, to reduce swelling.
Start with 1 teaspoon of dried herb per cup of boiling water, adjust as needed.
Combine Achillea millefolium tea with other herbal teas to enhance its effects.
Monitor your condition and adjust the dosage or frequency of usage as recommended.

preparations.herb_desc: Ginkgo biloba teas have been traditionally used to treat edema, a condition characterized by the accumulation of fluid in tissues, due to their anti-inflammatory and diuretic properties. The herbal preparation helps to treat edema by improving blood circulation, reducing swelling, and promoting the removal of excess fluids from the body. The bioactive constituents of Ginkgo biloba teas, including flavonoids, terpenoids, and bilobalide, contribute to their therapeutic effects by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory mediators and enhancing the activity of diuretic enzymes. The benefits of using Ginkgo biloba teas to treat edema include reduced swelling, improved circulation, and alleviated symptoms of discomfort and pain.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of boiling water and 1 teaspoon of dried Ginkgo biloba leaves.
Measure 1 tablespoon of loose-leaf dried Ginkgo biloba leaves or 2 tea bags into a tea infuser.
Steep the Ginkgo biloba leaves in the boiling water for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup and discard the leaves. Add honey or lemon to taste.
Drink 1-2 cups of Ginkgo biloba tea per day for edema relief, ideally after meals.
preparations.preparation_study: According to "Acta neuropathologica", Ginkgo biloba teas may be effective in reducing cerebral edema, as a study on rats showed that the extract of Ginkgo biloba decreased manifestations of cytotoxic edema in the brain's white matter.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Ginkgo biloba teas can be effective in treating edema, but they may cause side effects such as dizziness, headaches, and nausea due to their blood-thinning properties and interactions with blood pressure medications. To use Ginkgo biloba teas safely, individuals should start with small doses, avoid taking them with other medications that lower blood pressure, and monitor their blood pressure regularly to prevent potential complications.
preparation_usage: Drink 1 cup of Ginkgo biloba tea twice a day.
Start with 1 teaspoon of dried Ginkgo biloba per cup.
Gradually increase to 2 teaspoons if symptoms persist.
Drink tea for 3-6 months to observe edema reduction.
Consult a doctor if edema worsens or persists after 6 months.

preparations.herb_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra teas, made from the roots of the licorice plant, have been traditionally used to treat edema, a condition characterized by swelling caused by excess fluid retention. The anti-inflammatory and diuretic properties of these teas help to alleviate edema by reducing fluid retention and promoting the removal of excess fluids from the body. The bioactive constituents responsible for these effects include glycyrrhizin, a triterpenoid saponin, which has been shown to inhibit the activity of aldosterone, a hormone that regulates fluid balance, and flavonoids, which have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. Regular consumption of Glycyrrhiza glabra teas has been found to provide relief from edema symptoms, improve overall fluid balance, and reduce the risk of complications associated with this condition.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh or dried Glycyrrhiza glabra roots. Choose roots that are clean and free of mold.
Measure 1 tablespoon of the roots and chop them into small pieces. You can also use a root powder if available.
Boil 1 cup of water in a pot. Bring the water to a boil, then reduce the heat to a simmer.
Add the chopped roots or root powder to the simmering water. Let it steep for 5-7 minutes. Strain the tea into a cup using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth.
Drink the tea 2-3 times a day for edema relief. You can also add honey or lemon to taste, but make sure to consult a doctor before making any changes to your diet.
preparations.preparation_study: According to "Molecules (Basel, Switzerland)", Glycyrrhiza glabra teas for edema are suggested to be nontoxic and pose no health risk for both ingestion and topical application, based on the study's calculations of metal pollution index, hazard quotient, hazard index, and chronic daily intake.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Glycyrrhiza glabra teas can help alleviate edema symptoms, but using them for an extended period may lead to potential side effects such as water and electrolyte retention, high blood pressure, and an increased risk of potassium deficiency. To minimize these risks, individuals should monitor their blood pressure and electrolyte levels, consume a balanced diet, and avoid taking Glycyrrhiza glabra teas for more than a few weeks at a time.
preparation_usage: Consult a doctor before using Glycyrrhiza glabra tea for edema treatment.
Drink 1-2 cups of Glycyrrhiza glabra tea, 2-3 times a day.
Start with a low dosage of 1/2 teaspoon of dried root per cup of boiling water.
Increase dosage to 1 teaspoon per cup of boiling water if symptoms persist.
Continue using Glycyrrhiza glabra tea for 2-4 weeks to see noticeable improvements.

preparations.herb_desc: Urtica dioica teas, also known as stinging nettle tea, have been traditionally used to treat edema, a condition characterized by swelling due to excess fluid retention. The anti-inflammatory and diuretic properties of this herbal preparation help to reduce swelling and alleviate pain associated with edema. The bioactive constituents of Urtica dioica teas, including flavonoids, alkaloids, and glycosides, contribute to its therapeutic effects by promoting blood circulation, reducing fluid retention, and alleviating inflammation. The benefits of using Urtica dioica teas to treat edema include reduced swelling, improved circulation, and relief from associated pain and discomfort.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh Urtica dioica leaves, 1 cup of boiling water, and a tea strainer.
Chop the Urtica dioica leaves into small pieces and add them to the tea strainer.
Pour the boiling water over the Urtica dioica leaves and let it steep for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup and discard the Urtica dioica leaves.
Drink the Urtica dioica tea 2-3 times a day for edema relief.
preparations.preparation_study: N/A
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Urtica dioica teas can be used to treat edema, but it may cause side effects such as nausea, stomach upset, and allergic reactions in some individuals, particularly those who are sensitive to the plant's urticaria-inducing properties. To use Urtica dioica teas safely, it is recommended to start with a small dose and gradually increase it to avoid over-stimulation of the body, and also to monitor for signs of allergy or intolerance, such as skin rashes or itching, and discontinue use if they occur.
preparation_usage: Drink 1 cup of Urtica dioica tea 2-3 times a day.
Start with 1 teaspoon of dried leaves per cup of boiling water.
Gradually increase to 2-3 teaspoons if edema persists.
Use Urtica dioica tea for 3-6 months to see noticeable results.
Monitor edema reduction and adjust tea consumption as needed.

preparations.herb_desc: Taraxacum officinale teas, also known as dandelion tea, have been used to treat edema due to its anti-inflammatory and diuretic properties. The bioactive constituents present in this herbal preparation, including flavonoids, phenolic acids, and sesquiterpenes, help to reduce fluid retention and alleviate swelling in the affected areas. By promoting urine production and reducing water retention, Taraxacum officinale teas help to treat edema by flushing out excess fluids and reducing pressure on the body's tissues. The benefits of using Taraxacum officinale teas to treat edema include reduced swelling, improved circulation, and a decrease in the risk of complications associated with this condition.
preparation_procedure: Gather 2 tablespoons of dried Taraxacum officinale leaves and flowers.
Steep the Taraxacum officinale in 1 cup of boiling water for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the mixture into a cup using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth.
Add 1 tablespoon of honey (optional) to sweeten the tea.
Drink the tea 2-3 times a day for edema relief.
preparations.preparation_study: N/A
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Taraxacum officinale teas can be used to treat edema, but it may cause side effects such as digestive issues, allergic reactions, and interactions with certain medications. Precautions when using Taraxacum officinale teas include being cautious with dosages, avoiding use with other diuretics, and monitoring blood pressure levels, as excessive consumption can lead to dehydration and electrolyte imbalance.
preparation_usage: Drink 1-2 cups of Taraxacum officinale tea 2-3 times a day.
Start with 250-500 ml (1-2 cups) for 1-2 weeks to assess tolerance.
Gradually increase dosage to 500-750 ml (2-3 cups) if needed.
Consume Taraxacum officinale tea 2-3 hours before bedtime for better results.
Combine Taraxacum officinale tea with a healthy lifestyle and balanced diet for optimal benefits.

preparations.herb_desc: Angelica sinensis teas, also known as Dong Quai, have been used in traditional Chinese medicine to treat edema, a condition characterized by swelling due to excess fluid accumulation in the body. The anti-inflammatory and diuretic properties of Angelica sinensis teas help to reduce swelling and promote the removal of excess fluids from the body, thereby alleviating the symptoms of edema. The bioactive constituents of Angelica sinensis teas, including ferulic acid, caffeic acid, and ligustilide, contribute to its anti-inflammatory and diuretic effects, which help to treat edema. Regular consumption of Angelica sinensis teas has been shown to provide relief from edema symptoms, improve cardiovascular health, and reduce the risk of complications associated with this condition.
preparation_procedure: Gather 2 teaspoons of dried Angelica sinensis root and 1 cup of water in a pot.
Heat the water in the pot until it boils.
Add the dried Angelica sinensis root to the boiling water and let it steep for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup and discard the root.
Drink 1 cup of the tea 2-3 times a day to help with edema.
preparations.preparation_study: According to "Journal of alternative and complementary medicine (New York, N.Y.)", Angelica sinensis teas, specifically the extract known as Duhuo (Radix Angelicae Pubescentis), significantly reduced CFA-induced edema in rats at 24 hours, 72 hours, and 168 hours.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Angelica sinensis teas can cause dizziness, nausea, and stomach upset in some individuals, particularly when consumed in large quantities or for extended periods. Precautions should be taken when using Angelica sinensis teas to treat edema, including avoiding use during pregnancy and breastfeeding, as well as being mindful of potential interactions with blood-thinning medications.
preparation_usage: Drink 1-2 cups of Angelica sinensis tea 2-3 times a day.
Start with 0.5-1 teaspoon of dried Angelica sinensis root per cup.
Gradually increase to 1-2 teaspoons per cup if needed.
Continue for 1-2 weeks to assess the effectiveness.
Consult a healthcare professional if edema persists or worsens.

preparations.herb_desc: Astragalus membranaceus teas have been used in traditional medicine to treat edema, a condition characterized by swelling caused by excess fluid retention. preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of Vaccinium macrocarpon (cranberry) leaves, 1 cup of water, and a tea infuser or strainer.
Rinse the cranberry leaves with water, then dry them with a paper towel.
Steep 1 tablespoon of dried cranberry leaves in the cup of boiling water for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea using the infuser or strainer and discard the leaves.
Drink 1 cup of the tea 2-3 times a day to help with edema.
preparations.preparation_study: According to the provided study, Vaccinium macrocarpon teas for edema are not discussed, but it mentions that Vaccinium macrocarpon is one of the medicinal plants reported to have therapeutic potential for the management and cure of urinary tract infection (UTI) but no specific information about its effectiveness in treating edema is given.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Vaccinium macrocarpon teas can be used to treat edema, but it may cause side effects such as stomach upset, diarrhea, and allergic reactions in some individuals. Precautions should be taken when using Vaccinium macrocarpon teas, including monitoring blood sugar levels if you have diabetes, as it may affect blood sugar control, and avoiding it during pregnancy and breastfeeding due to potential hormonal effects.

preparations.herb_desc: Silybum marianum teas, also known as milk thistle teas, have been traditionally used to treat edema due to their anti-inflammatory and diuretic properties. The bioactive constituents of Silybum marianum, such as silymarin and flavonoids, help to treat edema by reducing fluid retention and improving blood circulation. Silybum marianum teas work by stimulating the kidneys to excrete excess fluids and electrolytes, thereby reducing swelling and alleviating the symptoms of edema. The benefits of using Silybum marianum teas to treat edema include improved circulation, reduced fluid retention, and a decrease in swelling, making it a natural and effective remedy for this common ailment.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of Silybum marianum flowers and 1 cup of boiling water.
Measure 1 tablespoon of dried Silybum marianum flowers and add to a tea infuser or a heat-resistant cup.
Pour 1 cup of boiling water over the flowers and let it steep for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea and discard the flowers. Add honey or lemon to taste, if needed.
Drink 1-2 cups of the Silybum marianum tea, 2-3 times a day, to help reduce edema.
preparations.preparation_study: According to Evidence-based complementary and alternative medicine : eCAM, Silybum marianum teas do not have information provided in the study regarding its effect on edema.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Silybum marianum teas can be used to treat edema due to its diuretic and anti-inflammatory properties, but it may cause side effects such as dizziness, nausea, and stomach upset in some individuals. Precautions should be taken when using Silybum marianum teas, including avoiding excessive consumption, as it may lead to dehydration and electrolyte imbalance.

preparations.herb_desc: Plantago lanceolata teas have been traditionally used to treat edema due to their anti-inflammatory and diuretic properties, which help to reduce swelling and fluid retention. The bioactive constituents present in these teas, such as flavonoids and phenolic acids, exhibit antioxidant and anti-inflammatory activities that contribute to their therapeutic effects. By promoting diuresis and reducing inflammation, Plantago lanceolata teas help to alleviate the symptoms of edema, including swelling, pain, and discomfort. The benefits of using these teas to treat edema include natural and non-invasive relief, reduced risk of side effects, and potential long-term management of the condition.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of dried Plantago lanceolata leaves and flowers.
Measure 1 tablespoon of the dried Plantago lanceolata and place it in a tea infuser or a heat-resistant cup.
Boil 1 cup of water and pour it over the Plantago lanceolata.
Let the mixture steep for 5-7 minutes, then strain the liquid.
Drink 1 cup of the tea 2-3 times a day to help relieve edema symptoms.
preparations.preparation_study: N/A
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Plantago lanceolata teas can be used as a potential treatment for edema, but consuming it may cause side effects such as nausea, dizziness, and stomach upset in some individuals due to its high concentration of tannins and other active compounds. To minimize potential risks, individuals should start with small doses, gradually increase as needed, and be cautious of interactions with other medications, particularly those affecting blood pressure and circulation.

preparations.herb_desc: Echinacea purpurea teas have been traditionally used to treat edema due to their anti-inflammatory and diuretic properties. The herbal preparation helps to reduce swelling and fluid retention by promoting the removal of excess fluids from the body, thereby alleviating the discomfort associated with edema. The bioactive constituents of Echinacea purpurea, including alkylamides, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, contribute to its therapeutic effects by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory mediators and enhancing the body's natural detoxification processes. Regular consumption of Echinacea purpurea teas may help to alleviate edema symptoms, improve circulation, and promote overall well-being.
preparation_procedure: Gather 2 tablespoons of dried Echinacea purpurea root and flowers.
Combine the dried Echinacea with 1 cup of boiling water in a tea infuser or a heat-resistant cup.
Steep the mixture for 5-7 minutes, then strain it into another cup.
Add honey or lemon to taste, if desired. The recommended dose is 1 cup of tea, 2-3 times a day.
Drink the tea immediately and store any leftover tea in the refrigerator for up to 24 hours.
preparations.preparation_study: According to Plant foods for human nutrition (Dordrecht, Netherlands), Echinacea purpurea teas for edema was not specifically mentioned in the provided study, which examined the clinical efficacy and safety of various herbal teas but did not focus on their use for edema.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Echinacea purpurea teas can be used to treat edema, but possible side effects include allergic reactions, gastrointestinal issues, and interactions with other medications, which can lead to symptoms such as hives, nausea, and dizziness. When using Echinacea purpurea teas to treat edema, it is essential to be aware of potential interactions with blood thinners and to monitor blood pressure, and to start with low doses to assess individual tolerance, and to avoid consuming it for extended periods.

preparations.herb_desc: Lithospermum erythrorhizon teas have been traditionally used to treat edema due to their diuretic and anti-inflammatory properties. The bioactive constituents, including lithospermic acid and lithospermone, help to increase urine production and reduce fluid retention, thus alleviating the symptoms of edema. The herbal preparation works by enhancing the kidneys' ability to filter and eliminate excess fluids, which in turn reduces swelling and discomfort associated with edema. Regular consumption of Lithospermum erythrorhizon teas has been reported to provide relief from edema symptoms and promote overall well-being.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of Lithospermum erythrorhizon roots and flowers. Clean them with water.
Dry the roots and flowers in a low-temperature oven (150°F) for 2 hours.
Grind 2 tablespoons of dried roots and flowers into a powder.
Steep 1 teaspoon of the powder in 1 cup of boiling water for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea and drink 1 cup, 2-3 times a day to help with edema.
preparations.preparation_study: N/A
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Lithospermum erythrorhizon teas can be used to treat edema, but its use may cause side effects such as dizziness, nausea, and allergic reactions in some individuals, particularly when consumed in large quantities or for extended periods. When using Lithospermum erythrorhizon teas to treat edema, it is essential to take precautions such as starting with small doses, monitoring body response, and avoiding consumption if you experience any adverse effects, as excessive use may exacerbate the condition or interact with other medications.

intro_study: According to the study "Complementary therapies in medicine", teas for edema may be effective in reducing lymphedema burden, particularly with the use of coumarin, a phytochemical reported in many studies to reduce edema volume.
